headline: 3 core Solana program addresses catalogued (SPL Stake Pool, JTO token, JitoSOL token); 1 cross-chain bridge contract; audit reports referenced; 45+ GitHub repositories documented.
short_headline: 4 addresses across Solana + Arbitrum; audits and repos identified

findings.text: Solana mainnet: SPL Stake Pool program SPoo1Ku8WFXoNDMHPsrGSTSG1Y47rzgn41SLUNakuHy (audited, upgrade-gated by Solana ecosystem committee). Governance token (JTO): jtojtomepa8beP8AuQc6eXt5FriJwfFMwQx2v2f9mCL. Liquid staking token (JitoSOL): J1toso1uCk3RLmjorhTtrVwY9HJ7X8V9yYac6Y7kGCPn. Devnet testnet addresses also identified.
Official deployments documentation at https://www.jito.network/docs/jitosol/jitosol-liquid-staking/security/deployed-programs/ lists core program addresses and notes upgrade-key governance structure.

SPL Stake Pool audits documented at https://spl.solana.com/stake-pool#security-audits with reports from Quantstamp, Neodyme (4 audits), Kudelski, Halborn, and OtterSec. Neodyme Jito-specific audit linked in protocol.audit_links (metadata fetched, body unavailable).

Jito Foundation GitHub organization (https://github.com/jito-foundation) contains 45 repositories including jito-stake-unstake-reference, restaking, stakenet, stake-deposit-interceptor, and jito-solana.

Protocol is Solana-native (single chain); no multi-chain deployments of core staking logic. Cross-chain bridge contract (StakePoolRate) identified on Arbitrum (0x8aa73ec870dc4a0af6b471937682a8fc3b8a21f8) via Wormhole.
SPL Stake Pool is not a factory; it is the canonical staking contract. No child pool enumeration required.

rationale.verdict: Catalogued 4 core contract addresses (3 on Solana, 1 on Arbitrum). All backed by official documentation, audit lists, and GitHub repositories. Upgrade governance is delegated to Solana ecosystem committee for the SPL Stake Pool, not controlled by Jito directly. Role taxonomy applied: stake pool program, governance token, liquid staking token, and bridge contract.

evidence.shows: SPL Stake Pool program address (SPoo1Ku8WFXoNDMHPsrGSTSG1Y47rzgn41SLUNakuHy) on Mainnet and Testnet, with links to audit reports from Quantstamp, Neodyme, Kudelski, OtterSec, and Halborn, including commit hashes.
JitoSOL liquid staking documentation confirming SPL Stake Pool program address and noting that upgrade keys are owned by a committee of Solana Staking Ecosystem participants.
Jito is a liquid staking service for Solana; Jito Stake Pool enables users to stake SOL for JitoSOL token; JitoSOL provides staking and MEV rewards.

JTO governance token address (jtojtomepa8beP8AuQc6eXt5FriJwfFMwQx2v2f9mCL), JitoSOL token address (J1toso1uCk3RLmjorhTtrVwY9HJ7X8V9yYac6Y7kGCPn), and description of StakeNet as decentralized stake pool manager.
Jito Foundation GitHub organization with 45 repositories including stake-deposit-interceptor, stakenet, jito-solana, jito-stake-unstake-reference, and restaking.
StakeNet is a decentralized Solana stake pool manager combining Validator History and Steward Programs. Links to validator history CLI and test documentation.
Reference repository for staking integration with manual DepositSol and WithdrawStake instruction building examples.
Universal framework for staking, restaking, and liquid restaking with Vault Program and Restaking Program components. TypeScript SDKs available.
StakePoolRate contract for JitoSOL deployed on Arbitrum at 0x8aa73ec870dc4a0af6b471937682a8fc3b8a21f8 using Wormhole Queries to provide Solana Stake Pool rate on EVM chain.

unknowns: D2-offchain: Full audit PDFs not fetched (Neodyme Jito PDF returned metadata only); content review deferred to evaluation slices

D4-github-commits: GitHub source repositories identified but commit SHAs not pinned to specific deployment versions; SHAs available in audit reports (e.g., Quantstamp 99914c9) but linking to live code vs. audit-era code requires follow-up

protocol_metadata.about: Jito is a liquid staking service for Solana that allows users to stake SOL in exchange for JitoSOL, a liquid staking token. The protocol distributes MEV rewards alongside standard staking rewards by delegating stake to validators running Jito-Solana client software. The core staking infrastructure uses the Solana Program Library (SPL) Stake Pool program, with upgrade authority held by a committee of Solana ecosystem participants. StakeNet provides autonomous, decentralized stake allocation management through Validator History and Steward programs.
